questions.text: Why is this Surah titled 'Al-Fatiha'?
questions.answer: It means 'The Opening,' as it is the first chapter of the Quran and the opening of the prayer (Salah).

questions.text: Name three other titles given to Surah Al-Fatiha.
questions.answer: Umm al-Kitab (Mother of the Book), As-Sab' al-Mathani (The Seven Oft-Repeated Verses), and Ash-Shifa (The Cure).

questions.text: What does the phrase 'Alhamdulillah' encompass beyond just 'praise'?
questions.answer: It combines both praise and gratitude, acknowledging that Allah is the source of all blessings and is inherently worthy of all glory.

questions.text: What is the meaning of 'Rabb al-Alamin'?
questions.answer: The Lord, Creator, Provider, and Sustainer of all the worlds (mankind, jinn, angels, and all existence).

questions.text: Linguistically, how does 'Ar-Rahman' differ from 'Ar-Rahim'?
questions.answer: Ar-Rahman refers to Allah's vast, all-encompassing mercy for all creation; Ar-Rahim refers to His specific, continuous mercy for the believers.

questions.text: What is the significance of the title 'Maalik-i Yawm id-Din'?
questions.answer: It establishes Allah as the Absolute King and Master of the Day of Judgment, where no one else will have authority.

questions.text: In 'Iyyaka Na'budu,' why is the word 'Iyyaka' (You) placed before the verb?
questions.answer: To signify 'exclusivity,' meaning 'You and only You do we worship.'

questions.text: What is the spiritual connection between 'Iyyaka Na'budu' (You we worship) and 'Iyyaka Nasta'in' (You we ask for help)?
questions.answer: It teaches that true worship is only possible through Allah's help, and we cannot fulfill our duties without His divine assistance.

questions.text: What is the definition of 'As-Sirat al-Mustaqim'?
questions.answer: The Straight Path; the clear, balanced way of Islam that leads directly to Allah's pleasure and Paradise.

questions.text: Who are 'those whom You have favored' (An'amta 'alayhim) according to other parts of the Quran?
questions.answer: The Prophets, the truthful ones (Siddiqin), the martyrs (Shuhada), and the righteous (Salihin).

questions.text: Who are 'those who have earned Your anger' (Al-Maghdubi 'alayhim)?
questions.answer: Those who knew the truth but intentionally rejected or disobeyed it.

questions.text: Who are 'those who have gone astray' (Ad-Dallin)?
questions.answer: Those who act without knowledge or follow their desires, wandering away from the truth.

questions.text: Is the word 'Amin' a part of the Quranic text of Surah Al-Fatiha?
questions.answer: No, it is not a verse of the Quran, but it is a Sunnah to say it after finishing the Surah, meaning 'O Allah, accept our prayer.'

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Fatiha divide between Allah and His servant according to a famous Hadith Qudsi?
questions.answer: The first half is praise and glorification for Allah, and the second half is a request and supplication for the servant's guidance.

questions.text: What is the legal ruling (Hukm) of reciting Al-Fatiha in the daily Salah?
questions.answer: It is a pillar (Rukn) of the prayer; the prayer is considered invalid if it is not recited.

questions.text: What core pillar of Aqeedah (belief) is established by 'Yawm id-Din'?
questions.answer: The belief in the Hereafter and the final accountability of all human actions.

questions.text: How does the Surah address the concept of 'Tawhid al-Uluhiyyah' (Oneness of Worship)?
questions.answer: Through the statement 'Iyyaka Na'budu,' which restricts all forms of worship to Allah alone.

questions.text: What does the plural form 'We' in 'We worship' and 'We ask for help' imply?
questions.answer: It emphasizes the concept of the 'Ummah' (community) and that believers are united in their worship and journey toward Allah.

questions.text: How does the Surah act as a summary of the entire Quran?
questions.answer: It contains the essence of Tawhid, the Day of Judgment, stories of past nations (favored vs. punished), and the path to guidance.

questions.text: What is the primary request we make to Allah in this Surah?
questions.answer: Guidance (Hidayah) to the straight path and the strength to remain firm upon it.
